title: sohmaea zonata
intro: Sohmaea zonata, also known as the fragrant wood fern or spotted wood fern, is a species of fern native to eastern Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sohmaea zonata include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as aiding in the treatment of fever and sore throats. The herbal preparations of Sohmaea zonata include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
